name: Hikule'o
description: Hikule'o is a significant deity in Tongan mythology, revered as the goddess of Pulotu, the underworld or afterlife. She is the guardian and ruler of this realm, a place where spirits of the deceased reside. Hikule'o is often depicted as an old woman, embodying wisdom and the cyclical nature of life and death. She holds immense power over the dead and is believed to possess knowledge of ancestral lineages and the secrets of the past. Some accounts portray her as a benevolent figure, overseeing the peaceful transition of souls, while others depict her as a formidable guardian, protecting Pulotu from unwanted intrusions. Hikule'o is also associated with the origins of certain crops and skills, suggesting a connection to fertility and the continuation of life. Her influence extends beyond the realm of the dead, impacting the living through dreams, omens, and the power of ancestral spirits. Respect for Hikule'o and the ancestors is a central tenet of Tongan culture, ensuring harmony between the living and the dead and maintaining the balance of the cosmos. She is a powerful figure, representing the mysteries of death and the enduring link between generations. She is believed to have immense power and the ability to influence the lives of the living. She is a central figure in Tongan cosmology and is deeply respected.
subtitle: Goddess of Pulotu, Tongan Underworld

keyMyths.title: Hikule'o and the Kava Ceremony
keyMyths.description: Hikule'o is said to have introduced the kava plant to the living world, symbolizing the connection between the living and the dead through ritual and tradition. The kava ceremony is a way to honor her and seek guidance from the ancestors. It is said she gifted this to the living through her connection with Eitumatupua.

keyMyths.title: Hikule'o's Domain in Pulotu
keyMyths.description: Pulotu, ruled by Hikule'o, is described as a lush and fertile land, a stark contrast to common Western depictions of the underworld. It is a place of rest and reflection for the spirits of the dead, where they await their journey back to the world of the living. It is a place where the ancestors reside and guide their descendants.

keyMyths.title: Hikule'o and the Origin of Life
keyMyths.description: Some myths suggest Hikule'o played a role in the creation of life, connecting her to the cycle of birth, death, and rebirth. She is seen as a source of life and fertility, ensuring the continuation of the Tongan people. This is often linked to the land and the bounty it provides.
